questions.question: What is the truth value of the statement 'p ∧ q' when p is true and q is false?
questions.explanation: The statement 'p ∧ q' is false when at least one of the statements is false, so when p is true and q is false, the statement is false.

questions.question: What is the truth value of the statement 'p ∨ q' when p is false and q is true?
questions.explanation: The statement 'p ∨ q' is true when at least one of the statements is true, so when p is false and q is true, the statement is true.

questions.question: What is the truth value of the statement '¬p' when p is true?
questions.explanation: The statement '¬p' is false when p is true, so when p is true, the statement is false.

questions.question: What is the truth value of the statement 'p ∧ q' when both p and q are true?
questions.explanation: The statement 'p ∧ q' is true when both statements are true, so when both p and q are true, the statement is true.

questions.question: What is the truth value of the statement 'p ∨ q' when both p and q are false?
questions.explanation: The statement 'p ∨ q' is false when both statements are false, so when both p and q are false, the statement is false.
